frameworks.name: phasic pupil-linked arousal
frameworks.one_line: pupil dilation indexes brainstem (LC-NE) arousal transients during decisions

frameworks.name: locus coeruleus-noradrenaline system
frameworks.one_line: central neuromodulatory source presumed to drive phasic arousal events

frameworks.name: decision formation / choice bias
frameworks.one_line: tests whether task-irrelevant arousal modulates perceptual decision bias

core_question: Can task-irrelevant auditory stimuli noninvasively manipulate phasic pupil-linked arousal in a temporally precise way and thereby alter perceptual decision formation?
key_assumptions: pupil size tracks phasic LC-NE arousal transients
task-evoked and externally-evoked pupil responses can superimpose linearly
challenging visual discrimination provides a sensitive testbed for arousal-bias coupling
manipulations: presence of task-irrelevant auditory sounds during visual decisions
onset latency of the sounds relative to stimulus/response
three separate experiments varying these parameters
key_findings: task-irrelevant sounds reliably elicited well-controlled pupil dilations
sound-evoked pupil responses superimposed onto task-evoked responses
previously reported negative correlation between task-evoked pupil and choice bias replicated
sound-driven pupil changes had no detectable effect on decision behavior
interpretations: cognitive task engagement and task-irrelevant sounds recruit dissociable neural systems controlling pupil size
pupil-linked arousal is not a single unitary signal causally driving decision bias
limitations_noted: task-irrelevant sounds did not produce the behavioral leverage needed for a clean causal manipulation
